title: The Ghost
content:
"Who knocks?" "I, who was beautiful,
    Beyond all dreams to restore,
 I, from the roots of the dark thorn am hither.
    And knock on the door."

 "Who speaks?" "I - once was my speech
    Sweet as the bird's on the air,
 When echo lurks by the waters to heed;
    'Tis I speak thee fair."

 "Dark is the hour!" "Ay, and cold."
    "Lone is my house." "Ah, but mine?"
 "Sight, touch, lips, eyes yearned in vain."
    "Long dead these to thine ..."

 Silence. Still faint on the porch
    Brake the flames of the stars.
 In gloom groped a hope-wearied hand
    Over keys, bolts, and bars.

 A face peered. All the grey night
    In chaos of vacancy shone;
 Nought but vast sorrow was there - 
    The sweet cheat gone.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
